commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR routes daily cache misses through `DomainDO`, adds in-memory coalescing for per-version/day generation, preserves `/stream` via a DO streaming path, stores fallback records on generation failure, and updates README/spec/tests.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main can generate outside the Durable Object on cold misses, and the PR blocker is also source-reproducible by following a `/stream` request whose upstream SSE emits a chunk and then throws. I did not run Cloudflare or local Wrangler in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can address the remaining stream failure/cache consistency blocker without changing the product direction of the PR.

target.title: [codex] Coalesce daily generation in Durable Object
target.body:
## Summary
- route cache misses through the per-host Durable Object get-or-create path
- coalesce concurrent daily misses with an in-memory in-flight promise keyed by version/date
- preserve `/stream` as a live streaming endpoint by moving streaming generation into the Durable Object
- store deterministic fallback text when AI generation fails so requests do not retry forever
- harden failure handling for thrown or malformed DO responses, whitespace-only AI output, and slow upstream calls
- remove unsupported `stale-while-revalidate` Cache API claims and update docs for versioned keys

## Why
The README/spec promise one model call per host per UTC day, but the Worker generated outside the Durable Object on cold misses. Since Cloudflare edge cache is per data center, simultaneous cold misses could all see an empty DO record and independently call AI Gateway. The old `/stream` path had the same bypass and could retry forever on failure.

The fix keeps the public `/stream` contract: a first uncached `/stream` request still streams live output. That streaming generation now runs inside the per-host Durable Object and sets the same in-flight record promise used by `/daily`, so concurrent `/` or `/stream` misses do not start duplicate upstream calls.

## Validation
- `pnpm check`
- `npx wrangler@4 deploy --dry-run`
- Local Wrangler behavior proof with `GATEWAY_BASE` pointed at a local OpenAI-compatible mock:
  - `/stream` on fresh `proof-live.test` returned `Transfer-Encoding: chunked`, `x-generated-on: 2026-06-17`, and body `# Mock stream page`
  - following `/` for the same host returned `CF-Cache-Status: HIT` and the same body
  - mock gateway call count stayed `0 -> 1 -> 1`
  - concurrent fresh-host `/stream` plus `/` returned `# Mock stream page` for both responses and call count moved only `2 -> 3`
target.author: vincent-peng

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

commands.author: vincent-peng
